meta.purpose: Define growth stages and measurable indicators for developing expertise in elderly services.
meta.load_condition: Load when the user asks how to improve elderly service skills or measure team progress.

stages.name: Program-Centric Provider
stages.description: Thinks in terms of programs, activities, and services. Measures success by program completion and attendance numbers. Sees seniors as program participants rather than individuals.
indicators: Describes work as 'running programs for the elderly'
Cannot name specific invisible barriers that keep people away
Blames low attendance on 'lack of interest' or 'marketing'

stages.name: Person-Aware Provider
stages.description: Understands that each senior is an individual with their own history, fears, and preferences. Begins to notice invisible barriers but doesn't yet systematically address them.
indicators: Knows individual names, backgrounds, and interests of participants
Can identify that someone has stopped coming but may not know why
Sometimes catches dignity threats but doesn't have systems to prevent them

stages.name: Dignity-First Designer
stages.description: Designs every interaction, program, and policy starting from dignity preservation. Anticipates invisible barriers before they arise. Creates psychological safety by default.
indicators: New program design starts with entry cost analysis, not activity planning
Exit processes capture invisible barriers that caused departure
Technology training normalizes mistakes and teaches error recovery
Language consistently uses identity-based framing, never age-based

stages.name: Dignity System Builder
stages.description: Builds organizational systems that encode dignity-first design so that quality does not depend on individual intuition. Trains others to see invisible barriers.
indicators: Staff across the organization use dignity-first language consistently
New hires develop barrier-detection skills within their first month
Program retention rates are measurably higher than industry average
Exit interview data directly feeds program redesign

evolution_layers.name: Individual Recognition
evolution_layers.capability: Ability to see each senior as a unique person with their own identity, history, and preferences rather than as a demographic category.

evolution_layers.name: Barrier Detection
evolution_layers.capability: Ability to identify invisible barriers (psychological entry cost, dignity threats, burden avoidance) that prevent engagement.

evolution_layers.name: Dignity Encoding
evolution_layers.capability: Ability to encode dignity-first design into organizational systems, training, and processes so that quality scales beyond individual practitioners.

measurement.what: Percentage of first-time participants who return for a second session
measurement.how: Track attendance data with first-timer flag. Calculate return rate within 30 days.
measurement.threshold: At least 60% of first-time participants return within 30 days.

measurement.what: Percentage of departures for which the actual reason for leaving is known
measurement.how: For each person who stops attending, record whether a personal follow-up identified the specific barrier.
measurement.threshold: Barrier identified for at least 80% of departures.

measurement.what: Frequency of banned demographic language in program communications
measurement.how: Audit all program materials, website, and staff communication for banned terms.
measurement.threshold: Zero occurrences of age-based demographic labeling in external communications.
